genre_refs: American West / Monument Valley landscape iconography
description: A designed slogan graphic by DHS using a sweeping photograph of a Southwestern desert landscape resembling Monument Valley: red-rock mesas, buttes and spires under a soft pastel pink-and-lavender dawn/dusk sky, with sandy desert plains stretching to the horizon. Centered in the upper-middle of the image, in a large white serif (slab/Western-style) typeface stacked on two lines, is the slogan 'Worth Fighting For.' There is no visible agency seal on the image itself. The aesthetic is warm, romantic and patriotic, evoking the grandeur of the American landscape and 'homeland' imagery. The messaging technique is emotional/patriotic appeal: pairing majestic natural scenery with a terse call-to-arms slogan to frame the nation as something sacred that must be defended. The accompanying tweet text reinforces this: 'God has blessed us to call the greatest nation in history home. We must fight for America.' Target audience: the broad public and DHS supporters, cultivating nationalist sentiment and implicit justification for aggressive immigration/homeland enforcement.
